description: A commercial facility in Anchorage, Alaska is considering a 250 kW DC rooftop solar installation. Quantify the annual energy loss caused by snow cover for a 250 kW DC fixed-tilt PV system (45 degree tilt, 180 degree azimuth) using the weather data at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/Anchorage.epw. Perform simulations to determine the difference between a baseline (snow model disabled) and a snow-enabled configuration. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/snow_loss_analysis.json using the following structure: {"baseline_energy_kwh": <float>, "snow_model_energy_kwh": <float>, "snow_loss_kwh": <float>, "snow_loss_percentage": <float>}. Additionally, save your Python script (.py) or SAM project file (.sam) in the /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/ directory as evidence of your workflow.
